name: Pearl Bailey
abstract: Pearl Mae Bailey (March 29, 1918 – August 17, 1990) was an American actress, singer, comedian and author. After appearing in vaudeville, she made her Broadway debut in St.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I felt that when she passed away... was a big shock to all of us... She was a great lady, great performer for 38, almost 39 years... we decided to get married... I was musical director for his wife Pearl Bailey
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I was married to Pearl at that time... I had Earl Palmer play drums when Pearl did her television shows. She did 15 shows. I conducted the show. I had Ray Brown on bass, Earl Palmer on drums
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I always dreamed that one day I'd probably get a chance to play in his band -- which did happen when Louis Bellson went to England to marry Pearl Bailey; I took his place in the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Pearl Bailey and the band on the road with Louie.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Pearl Bailey only in the context of her relationship with Louie Bellson ('Louie was getting ready to go to London to meet Pearl to marry her') and her residence at Tizol's house. There is no direct interaction or personal connection described between the interviewee and Pea

about_quotes.speaker: Noah Evans
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer asks 'Tell me about working with Pearl Bailey,' but the interviewee (Gil Evans) does not respond to this specific question in the provided transcript, instead continuing to discuss Benny Goodman.
